entries.title: How to Get Your Team to Actually Use AI
entries.quote: Three pillars: Define, Measure, Facilitate. Sustained behavior change is the real goal.
entries.notes: LinkedIn Newsletter edition (subscribers notified). Source file: brain/the-build-room/published-content/how-we-measure-impact-linkedin.md. L&D variant ready for republish closer to Learning Live.

entries.title: AI and the Devil: How to Understand Our Part in The Existential Threat
entries.quote: Existential / ethical lens on AI. Uses social media algorithms as the cautionary frame for AI's societal impact. Pairs with the Stay Human thread of The Build Room.
entries.notes: LinkedIn Newsletter edition.

entries.title: Your Meetings Are Probably Costing You Millions
entries.quote: Operations / leadership angle. Opens with a $3M case study on inefficient meeting structures.
entries.notes: LinkedIn Newsletter edition. Useful pair with AI fluency reading list.

entries.title: Thesis line, on camera: Learn AI. Build Robots. Stay Human.
entries.quote: I like to say learn AI, build robots and stay human. And stay human is a big part of the build cohort that we're working together.
entries.notes: Cornerstone clip for Build Room. Auto-top per clip-candidates brief. Source: webinar-2026-06-17 [30:19-30:31]. Cut as standalone reel still TODO.

entries.title: BC and AC: Before Cruise and After Cruise
entries.quote: My hope is that in your mind there's BC and AC, before cruise and after cruise in your AI journey, and that a few months after the cruise you point back and you say, that was a turning point for me in my AI journey. I built something. I connected with people and now my life is kind of changed for it.
entries.notes: Single best vision moment of the entire webinar. 30s LinkedIn ad candidate. TODO: cut clip from webinar.

entries.title: Operators and multipliers are the most isolated people
entries.quote: I'm finding that the people who are in that operator multiplier category are some of the most isolated people because no one's up on their level yet. So for us to get you together, I think it's going to be really valuable.
entries.notes: Reframes the offer for advanced builders who'd self-disqualify. TODO: cut clip.

entries.title: Losing my ambitious friends to AI
entries.quote: I'm here in Medellin Colombia and the community around me, I feel like I'm losing some of my more ambitious friends to AI. 'Alex, I'm so revved up on AI. I've built so much. It's transformed my company, but I haven't been to the gym. I haven't hung out with my friends. I haven't called my mom.' Do it with us. Do it with family. Do it with community.
entries.notes: The Stay Human thesis told as a story. Most emotionally resonant Build Room moment in the webinar. TODO: cut as long-form reel.

entries.title: The four levels: explore, adopt, operator, multiplier
entries.quote: I rate AI use and fluency on four levels. So it's explore — that's basically I play with, I chat with the bot. Adopter — it's coming into my everyday. But we move into operator and multiplier. Operators are people who are building tools already. And then there's even another level called multiplier, which is what I do impacts other people.
entries.notes: Original framework, Alex's own IP. Reusable across every Build Room asset. TODO: cut clip + carousel.

entries.title: Build sessions: hands on keyboard, get things done
entries.quote: I'm really excited for the build sessions especially because this is where we put our hands on the keyboard and get things done. That's the most important thing.
entries.notes: Core Build Room positioning: doing, not watching.

entries.title: Build a tool for the first time, surrounded by coaches
entries.quote: For those of you who are beginning and you've kind of been using ChatGPT like Google or your friend and therapist, cool. We're going to move you to a place where you're building a tool for the first time. We're going to have you surrounded by coaches and friends.
entries.notes: Sells the on-ramp for beginners.

entries.title: Everyone leaves the ship with something they built
entries.quote: It's our goal to have everyone produce a tool while they're on the boat made from AI. / Everyone can leave the ship with something that they created, something new.
entries.notes: Two voices same promise back-to-back. Best split-cut reel candidate. Alex's version at [04:32-04:36]; Dori's at [12:00-12:08]. Consideration: dual-attribution; pick one timestamp as primary, hold both in body. TODO: cut split-cut clip.

entries.title: Following the Titanic route on a vintage ocean liner
entries.quote: Kunard used to be one of the main competitors of the Titanic company. When we are going on the ship we're really going back in time, pretty much following exactly the Titanic route. That has been a bucket list item for many people.
entries.notes: Strongest cruise-specific romance hook. Consideration: cohort=nc17 because it sells the cruise specifically, not transferable to other Build Room offers.

entries.title: Ed Sheeran recorded part of Divide on this ship
entries.quote: Ed Sheeran had a temporary studio created on the Queen Mary 2 to record part of an album. The album was called Divide.
entries.notes: Pop-culture hook. Instantly shareable. Consideration: pure cruise marketing, not Build Room thesis.

entries.title: Past Statue of Liberty into NYC at dawn
entries.quote: Arrival in New York in the morning. Early arrival past the Statue of Liberty. That's also something really really unique.
entries.notes: Cinematic moment, sells the ending. Needs Statue of Liberty b-roll.

entries.title: 150 people, no ports, no distractions
entries.quote: The sea days are absolutely my favorite. No ports, no excursions, no distraction whatsoever. We're all going to be let's say stuck on this boat but in the best way possible. It's like 150 people, so it's a small a little bit smaller group.
entries.notes: Sells the format: forced focus = the actual product. Consideration: framing (no distractions) transfers to Build Room cohort design, but this specific quote stays cohort=nc17.

entries.title: Two tracks running parallel: users and builders
entries.quote: These two tracks are going to run parallel. So everyone is going to find whatever their level is. So no one needs to sit on sessions that might not be as interesting or might be a little bit too high level for them.
entries.notes: Addresses the #1 objection (am I the right level?). Carousel post or 25s explainer.

entries.title: Either we learn AI or we lose this lifestyle
entries.quote: If we are not using this, a lot of us who are doing this right now and who are not going into this, we're not going to be able to have certain jobs anymore. So either we learn how to use these tools, or we might not be able to run this kind of lifestyle anymore.
entries.notes: Urgency framing aimed at the nomad audience. Consideration: marked cross-cohort because the message travels beyond the cruise, even though the speaker is the cruise founder.

entries.title: Price goes up Wednesday; single cabins almost gone
entries.quote: Those of you who have not booked this yet, it's really important to get on board now. There are very very limited spots. This will sell out still within the next month or so.
entries.notes: Direct-response urgency. Consideration: time-sensitive; the urgency framing decays after sell-out. Pair with affiliate link.

entries.title: Like a vintage five-star hotel, not a cruise ship
entries.quote: You don't really feel like being on a cruise ship. You more feel like in a vintage five-star hotel.
entries.notes: Reframes objection for people who 'don't do cruises.'

entries.title: Moving you up a level of AI fluency
entries.quote: This last year has been a year of facilitating AI workshops. So taking people from whatever level of AI fluency and practice they're at and moving up, moving them up a level. And it's our goal to have everyone produce a tool while they're on the boat made from AI.
entries.notes: Concise Alex bio + Build Room promise in one bite. 20s LinkedIn intro reel candidate.

entries.title: Continuing the build in Playa: refine, expand, publish
entries.quote: We can continue to do facilitated AI build sessions. So you come off the cruise, you've built a tool and then you come work with us and you refine it, you expand it, maybe you publish it, maybe you start advertising and bringing new people into it. Maybe it's your new business.
entries.notes: Sells the cohort arc beyond a single event. Build Room's 'what's next' wedge. Consideration: also sells Alex's PDC retreat (post-cruise), so pulls double duty.

entries.title: Themed tracks: brand voice, ops, personal development
entries.quote: Are you a person who's trying to get your brand voice, there's a track for that. If you're a person who's trying to get your internal operations of your business together, there's a track for that. If you're a person who's trying to build personal development tools, there's a track for that.
entries.notes: Concrete 'what would I actually build' answer. Carousel or 30s explainer.

entries.title: Operators trade notes: you guys need to confer
entries.quote: We're going to have ways for the people who are in that operator multiplier level who are already so advanced for them to confer because you guys need to trade notes.
entries.notes: Direct call to advanced builders. Pair with the four-levels framework clip.

entries.title: Charlie Chaplin, Liz Taylor, and the 1920s ocean liners
entries.quote: This ocean liner has been operating since the 1920s. A lot of stars, they didn't take the plane, they were just taking this. There was Charlie Chaplin and Liz Taylor and a lot of politicians. You really get this feeling that it is something quite unique.
entries.notes: Romance / history porn. Aspirational nostalgia. Needs archival photos.

entries.title: Accept the FOMO
entries.quote: We recommend not to join everything. Just really accept the FOMO. There's no need to be everywhere. Take it easy, take it on your own pace, because otherwise it can be quite overwhelming.
entries.notes: Counterintuitive, human, very on-brand for Stay Human. Consideration: marked cross-cohort because the message extends past the cruise.

entries.title: The roommate became my bestie
entries.quote: The roommate turned into my bestie. I was just hanging out with my very first roommate here, Virginia. This is just such a special bond and a special friendship that creates when you are sharing a cabin.
entries.notes: Soft, human, community-driven. Counterweight to AI content.

entries.title: Pitch your friend, not yourself
entries.quote: Instead of pitching up and talking about yourself, you can pitch your friends. It was super super popular. People loved it.
entries.notes: Charming, novel format. Differentiates the cruise's culture.

entries.title: Tacos, tacos, tacos for me
entries.quote: Every place in Playa Carmen is about a 10-minute walk from the beach, and tacos, tacos, tacos for me. Tacos for lunch, tacos for dinner. They're like 20 pesos, about a dollar and a half.
entries.notes: Personality moment. Alex being playful. Good organic-feeling reel. Sells the post-cruise Playa extension.

entries.title: Cenotes: healing water in the jungle
entries.quote: There are these beautiful natural jungle lakes basically that have opened up from underground channels and the water is a mineral water. Some people in that area believe it's a sort of a healing water as well.
entries.notes: Sensory, exotic. Sells the after-cruise extension.

entries.title: One tip can save you 5-6 hours
entries.quote: Sometimes you just need to have this one little tip that can really move you forward. Someone shared a tool with me yesterday that I never heard of before. It basically saves me five hours, six hours easily of time.
entries.notes: Personal proof for 'why we do this together' framing. Cross-cohort because the message is about peer learning generally.

entries.title: Stuck on this boat in the best way possible
entries.quote: We're all going to be, let's say, stuck on this boat but in the best way possible.
entries.notes: Punchy single-line stinger. 8-12s reel ender.

entries.title: Dori at 1am, Alex at noon, Johannes at 7pm
entries.quote: Dori, what time is it where you are? / Don't even ask. It's 1am. Past 1am. / Alex: Just past noon. Almost lunchtime. / Johannes: Here it's just past 7pm in Barcelona. So we are really all over the world.
entries.notes: Lived-in proof of the nomad lifestyle the cruise sells. Consideration: three-speaker clip, harder to cut clean.

entries.title: The first hour is the hardest. Burn the thread when stuck.
entries.quote: The first hour is the hardest. You're not a week in, you're an hour in. And when Claude gets anchored to a broken precedent, you have permission to burn the thread and start fresh.
entries.notes: Theme of DBALP S2. Captured in the recap intro. Consideration: overlaps with the standalone 'burn the thread' lesson entry but adds the 'first hour is the hardest' framing — keep both, they serve different uses.

entries.title: I'm on purpose. I'm on track.
entries.quote: I'm on purpose. I'm on track.
entries.notes: Permission NOT yet asked. Sat unused for months because no one asked. Named explicitly in CUSTOMER-JOURNEY.md as the canonical example of the testimonial-form [GAP]. Action: build the testimonial form, then ask Ruben.

entries.title: Kate's Missive sidebar shipped live, public, mid-session
entries.quote: Your sidebar is live. Public. Today. DA. UVPM. Outlet. Reporter. Email. Beats. Macro URL. All of it in one panel, inside Missive. You wired Monday into the same flow during the session. Save to Monday Fox Hollow works. Bryce said it out loud: this is the thing we hired a developer for, for two months. You built it in three sessions.
entries.notes: FEATURED candidate for marketing once permission asked. Mid-session continuous extension pattern (Bryce asked, Kate shipped same session, room applauded). TODO: ask Kate for permission to use as marketing asset; pull video clip from S3 Fathom https://fathom.video/share/niM3mdCCwSSFEhbyJyMjfMaAxQgNM32A.

entries.title: Bryce's callback: remember when we hired that developer for two months
entries.quote: Remember when we hired that developer for two months.
entries.notes: Said during Kate's Missive sidebar live-build moment. Paraphrase, not verbatim. Pulls double duty as buyer testimonial (org-buyer recognizing ROI live). TODO: confirm exact wording from S3 transcript.

entries.title: Bryce's CRM with cats and sad dogs in breakup emails
entries.quote: You went from 'I want to never answer a sales email again' to a working CRM that drafts responses, updates itself, and uses cats and sad dogs in the breakup emails. That's not a prototype. That's in production.
entries.notes: Strong Demo Day candidate. The owner himself built it.

entries.title: Auds: that's not a dashboard, that's a tool with a user
entries.quote: My main goal is to give something the CMs can use, that they feel confident pulls the insights and the analytics in a way they can talk about on the call.
entries.notes: Said the line the whole cohort needed. Tool-with-a-user framing reusable across cohorts.

entries.title: Ehdina stopped fighting make.com and walked back to the meat
entries.quote: You stopped fighting make.com. You walked back to the meat of the problem. You built a Claude project where you say 'draft this' and out comes the LinkedIn post, the IG caption, and a voice check. Then you went one further: you're adding an instruction to test the output against the voice automatically.
entries.notes: Build-the-meat-wrap-with-logistics-later heuristic in action.

entries.title: Marley found the bug PRVault didn't know they had
entries.quote: You pivoted from scraping the platform to working with the CSV export. Faster. Cleaner. Then you sat with Bryce and built the temporary pipeline: PRVault → email → Slack → CM → Pitchy. Manual for now, automated later. The discipline of doing the smallest version first is the whole game.

entries.title: Kandice consolidated 10-15 lists per campaign into ONE
entries.quote: You consolidated 10-15 lists per campaign into ONE list. That's not a feature. That's a change in how the team works. Your backend is the most ambitious build in this cohort.
entries.notes: Confidence-gap watch per s3-ledger-health. Said 'I got nothing to show yet' despite shipping the most ambitious backend in the cohort.

entries.title: Chanel hit a Monday API wall and didn't quit
entries.quote: You hit a Monday API wall and you didn't quit. You came back, paired with Auds, and pivoted to a dashboard you're now refining.

entries.title: Kate: tell me the rules that you're learning from me
entries.quote: Tell me the rules that you're learning from me.
entries.notes: The Build Plan Loop in Kate's own words. Reusable framework moment.

entries.title: Chanel burned the polluted thread and rebuilt in 15 minutes
entries.quote: I feel better now.
entries.notes: The line at end of session after the rebuild. Pairs with the cross-cohort 'burn the thread' lesson.

entries.title: Auds shared a live working dashboard at share-out
entries.quote: Shared a live working dashboard at share-out. First visible fragment of where this whole cohort is heading.

entries.title: Marley discovered CSV export works cleaner than screenshots
entries.quote: Discovered CSV export works cleaner than screenshots. Surfaced real gaps in the existing reporting template while building.
entries.notes: Earlier session of the same pivot that landed in S3.

entries.title: Ehdina chained Make + Claude + Drive + Notion after Copy.ai paywall
entries.quote: Scenarios configured and running. Strong autonomous progress with minimal support needed.

entries.title: Kandice showed up sick and got unblocked anyway
entries.quote: Showed up sick and still got unblocked. MongoDB access established via Bryce.

entries.title: Bryce handed out API keys, MongoDB, Pitchy templates live
entries.quote: Provided API keys, MongoDB access, and Pitchy templates live so other builders could keep moving.
entries.notes: Owner-buyer in the room building AND co-facilitating. Story for org-buyer marketing.

entries.title: David shipped a working Zoom downloader UI before S2
entries.quote: You showed up with a working tool already built. Clicking a button on your machine downloads the audio, transcript, and video for any Zoom session you point it at. That's not S2 work, that's bonus.

entries.title: Anonymize transcripts before LLM analysis
entries.quote: Best moment of the session was your idea to anonymize transcripts before sending them through the LLM. Strip the client names, replace with 'client,' then evaluate. Cleaner privacy story, same coaching signal. Stealing that for the whole cohort.
entries.notes: Promoted cohort-wide as Foundations module for S3. Applies to David's eval tool, Jessica's clip search, Maho's portal.

entries.title: Maho: can we do it personalized for them?
entries.quote: Can we do it personalized for them?
entries.notes: Smartest question of the day. Scope-locked the right S2 shape: local web page with client dropdown pulling real data from GHL per client. Stretch (full portal with login) lives at S4.

entries.title: Cata pivoted to Google Drive when GHL was blocked
entries.quote: Pivot for S2: connect Codex to Google Drive, point it at the onboarding resources folder, get it to answer 'how do I log into Kajabi?' with the real link. The laptop situation set you back on GHL, but your project doesn't actually need GHL right now.

entries.title: Muhammad's brand-voice content engine via Slack
entries.quote: You made a real call this session. Skip Google Drive, integrate Slack instead. Team writes a title, Codex drafts the post in RPA voice, it lands in Slack for human review before publishing.

entries.title: Jessica picked up the clip-database project mid-session
entries.quote: You picked up a real project mid-session. Find a webinar transcript, return the timestamps where good clips live. That's hard and useful.
entries.notes: Jessica is also Build Room coach + facilitator. Pairs with Maho on Zoom credentials.

entries.title: Majo connected Codex to GoHighLevel and pulled real client data
entries.quote: Connected Codex to GoHighLevel and pulled real client data. First brick laid.

entries.title: Kata's Codex exploration surfaced a real calendar sync bug
entries.quote: Codex connected to GHL. Bonus: while exploring, it diagnosed a calendar sync bug on Alicia's account, a real problem the team was already living with.
entries.notes: Recap doc says 'Kata' but per spelling rule the cohort participant is Cata. Silent correction.

entries.title: David fought through Zoom API scopes and pulled a test recording
entries.quote: Connected Codex to Zoom via the marketplace, fought through API scopes (calendar + meetings + recording), and pulled down a test recording by end of session.

entries.title: Muhammad locked scope: train on RPA voice so it doesn't sound generic
entries.quote: Scope locked. MVP is voice-training on RPA's existing content so it doesn't sound like generic AI.

entries.title: Bernie narrowed 5 features to 1 MVP in under 60 seconds
entries.quote: Probably checking into, I hate doing billing, so I'd love to pull data from PayPal.
entries.notes: Cleanest Backwards Design demo in S1. Featured candidate for the first-brick teaching moment.

entries.title: Armin: a full force marketing team so I never have to hire again
entries.quote: I want a full force marketing team so I never have to hire someone like this again.

entries.title: Steve: finance is the most controlled thing in the world
entries.quote: Finance is about the most controlled thing in the world. Would you shy away from that because of the interconnects and security and all that?
entries.notes: Reframed to start with his own finances first. Safer first brick, same project.

entries.title: Álvaro's plan-in-ChatGPT, save-as-markdown, point-Codex-at-folder workflow
entries.quote: Plan in ChatGPT, request a markdown document instead of a prompt, save the markdown to a folder, open with VS Code, point Codex at the folder. I can generate more markdowns that carry more context across different chats.
entries.attribution: Álvaro José Castaño Mendoza (F&F Mon cohort)
entries.notes: A builder teaching builders on session one. Cohort-wide reusable workflow.

entries.title: Jordan: when to use chat vs code to maximize usage
entries.quote: When to use things like chat versus code to be able to maximize usage.
entries.notes: Reframed the next twenty minutes for the whole cohort.

entries.title: Alejandro's tooling question IS his MVP
entries.quote: How you would include the, what people sometimes reverse like AI brains, like if you have it linked to Notion or Google Drive or OneDrive, so that it kind of collects the data that it can pull from.
entries.notes: Building a second-brain repo for psychedelic integration. Question + MVP collapse into one line.

entries.title: Thierry: talk before executing to save tokens
entries.quote: I just talk before executing. I would do a couple of iterations of developing the idea with text and then only then implement. Or you can also split your files into separate sections.
entries.notes: Coach credibility established on call one.

entries.title: Jessica's plan-in-ChatGPT to build-in-Codex handoff
entries.quote: I usually start by saying, hey, I have these project ideas that we're going to execute in Codex, but you are helping me to plan it.
entries.attribution: Jessica Tovar Benalcázar (F&F Mon cohort coach)

entries.title: Jeremy named his blocker out loud on session 1
entries.quote: I can open Claude Code, it's just not hooking up with GitHub.
entries.notes: Not glamorous but the unstuck moment that lets the project start.

entries.title: Ludger put the full video-automation dream on the table
entries.quote: An automation for video generation, calendar timelines, interview cuts, talking dogs, lip-sync, image generation.
entries.notes: Full ambition visible to the room. Narrowing is the next conversation.

entries.title: Burn the thread when Claude is anchored to broken precedent
entries.quote: When Claude gets anchored to a broken precedent, you have permission to burn the thread and start fresh.
entries.notes: Theme of DBALP S2. Reusable as a Build Room heuristic.

entries.title: Build the meat, wrap with logistics later
entries.quote: Build the meat, wrap with logistics later.
entries.notes: Promoted to named curriculum heuristic in DBALP S3 debrief (WORKBENCH 2026-06-23). Surfaced with Ehdina (drop make.com, paste transcript manually) and Marley (work the CSV core, don't wait on PRVault fix).

entries.title: First brick: don't try to build the cathedral
entries.quote: Don't try to build the cathedral. Get one real API integration working, then push from there.
entries.notes: Theme of RPA S1. Foundational Build Room lesson.

entries.title: Threads burn. Files remain.
entries.quote: Threads burn. Files remain.
entries.notes: Theme of RPA S2 per s2-wins-post. Already live as a standalone visual in build-room-visuals.

entries.title: Kate shipped the Missive sidebar live and public
entries.quote: I am pretty done on the tool. ... I made it public today, earlier today.
entries.notes: DA + UVPM + outlet + reporter + email + beats + macro URL in one Missive sidebar panel by S3. Cohort leader.

entries.title: Kate ships peer-suggested feature mid-session, room applauds
entries.quote: Bryce: could you connect Monday to this, Kate, and pull more conversations? Kate at 01:51: I created like this opportunity overview ... this button is working, like save the Monday Fox Hollow form. Bryce: remember when we hired that developer for like two months to do this for us? Yeah, Bryce, this is that, this is like that too.
entries.timestamp: 00:34:20 idea then 01:51:00 working button then 01:52:43 developer callback
entries.notes: Marketing-clip candidate. Three-beat moment: peer drops idea, builder ships inside session, CEO does the ROI callback. New CUSTOMER-JOURNEY touchpoint pattern lifted from this. Needs permission ask before public use.

entries.title: Bryce sales auto-responder is in production
entries.quote: I put the sales agent kind of to all my inboxes and all my calendars ... helps me draft up responses to leads coming in, whether they are new leads or sales proposals or people who have ghosted me. I built a lot of playbooks on how to respond, when to respond, the cadence, what to send. ... pretty much that is finished.
entries.notes: Original cohort promise was Never answer another sales email ever again. Delivered by S3.

entries.title: Bryce on humanizing breakup emails with confused cats and sad dogs
entries.quote: Instead of sending breakup emails, which is the thing to do after no one responds, I am just sitting on hats now ... and cats doing stuff, which works, honestly works so well. It just breaks the frame a little bit more. So like that confused cat and a sad dog. ... So it is like getting rid of the standard sales practices, what everyone does and adding a little humor.
entries.notes: Concrete Stay Human moment from a builder. Strong landing-page candidate for Learn AI. Build Robots. Stay Human. Needs permission.

entries.title: Ehdina dropped make.com, built a Claude project that drafts post + caption + voice check
entries.quote: I created a new cloud project, but it already runs everything ... I was too focused on the automation when I can literally manually upload the transcript or whatever asset. So we have to pivot to actually just creating the actual post and captions. ... I just have to say draft this and it would give me the link post, the IG caption and the voice check.
entries.notes: Live pivot in S3 from automation chasing to building the meat. Then self-identified next refinement (voice self-check).

entries.title: Kandice reporter tool consolidates 10-15 lists per campaign into 1
entries.quote: Normally for the campaigns, we will upload like 10 to 15 lists per client per campaign. ... but this way we will only have to upload one list for the campaign because it will have everything.
entries.notes: Most ambitious backend in cohort. Builder undersold to peers (I got nothing to show yet) even after stating this consolidation out loud. The story IS the consolidation.

entries.title: Chanel on the platform-limitation burn
entries.quote: He used to do this to me. I remember spending two days on something, and then at the very end, it was like, oh, yeah, this platform does not support it.
entries.notes: Honest cohort emotion. Useful proof that pivot pain is normal, not a Build Room failure. Followed by Alex framing AI tools can help us break out of the frame.

entries.title: Auds names her user out loud, the CM on a client call
entries.quote: My main goal is to try to give something that the CMs will be able to share with their clients. Even if it looks a little messy, I am less concerned about that. I really just want them to feel confident that it pulls the insights and the analytics in a way that they can then talk about on the call.
entries.notes: Best user-story articulation of the DBALP cohort. Useful for tools with a user, not toys framing.

entries.title: Build the meat first. Wrap with logistics later.
entries.quote: When I am prototyping, I want to work on the meatiest thing first. ... She said, well, I want it to be password protected. And I said, let us do that later because we know how to do that. Because if you make a password system and there is no dashboard inside, we made the password system for nothing. So we make the dashboard first. We make the core of the thing first, and then we wrap the things around it as needed.
entries.notes: Surfaced live in DBALP S3 to redirect Ehdina off make.com integration and toward output quality. Same shape applied with Marley (work the CSV core, do not wait on PRVault fix). Promote to a named curriculum heuristic.

entries.title: Bryce: remember when we hired that developer for two months to do this for us? This is that.
entries.quote: Remember when we hired that developer for like two months to do this for us? Yeah, Bryce, this is that, this is like that too.
entries.attribution: Bryce (DBALP cohort, CEO) about Kate Missive sidebar
entries.notes: CEO ROI callback, in-cohort, unprompted. Useful for org-buyer landing copy. Same moment as dbalp-s3-kate-mid-session-extension; isolated as a quote for reuse. Needs permission.

entries.title: Ruben locked MVP + 2-hour completion window on call one
entries.quote: I'm going be taking sample financial data... write out code that calculates the sequence of payments day by day until debt is zero. I'm going finish doing it in two hours.
entries.attribution: Ruben Rosado (F&F Humans V Robots Thu cohort)
entries.notes: Deep IP. Confidentiality requested + agreed on call. Do NOT clip externally without explicit Ruben permission. Fathom: https://fathom.video/share/MxD5y66n8ETNFjHWPeqSa2dLDkH_cYnu

entries.title: Cece's first Zoom since COVID → working ChatGPT photo-rating system in one session
entries.quote: It just happened so fast. It was pretty awesome.
entries.attribution: Cece Bedolla (F&F Humans V Robots Thu cohort)
entries.notes: Speaker mis-attributed to Dominika in Fathom transcript; content matches Cece's photo-curator project from earlier in call. TODO: cut clip from https://fathom.video/share/MxD5y66n8ETNFjHWPeqSa2dLDkH_cYnu around 01:56.

entries.title: Anthony stayed after formal close to keep narrowing scope on Google Ads alert agent
entries.quote: So I'm going to try to, because I have my two screens, that's why I stayed at home.
entries.attribution: Anthony Clery (F&F Humans V Robots Thu cohort)
entries.notes: Builder-driven extension. Pattern worth surfacing in future cohort norms. Fathom: https://fathom.video/share/MxD5y66n8ETNFjHWPeqSa2dLDkH_cYnu

entries.title: Marie named the tedious task that IS the first brick
entries.quote: Just the time spent scrolling, searching, trying to identify if it's worth it or not.
entries.attribution: Marie (F&F Humans V Robots Thu cohort)
entries.notes: Job-board scraper for video editing roles. Fathom: https://fathom.video/share/MxD5y66n8ETNFjHWPeqSa2dLDkH_cYnu

entries.title: Zach shipped a self-tested coaching assessment form inside the build hour
entries.quote: I built the whole report. I built all the questions. I went through it myself, and it was powerful.
entries.attribution: Zach Cohen (F&F Humans V Robots Thu cohort)
entries.notes: Lead-magnet coaching assessment. From concept to filled-out form in ~40 minutes. Fathom: https://fathom.video/share/MxD5y66n8ETNFjHWPeqSa2dLDkH_cYnu

entries.title: Sam built + demoed a working audio web app with live filter controls in under one hour
entries.quote: Less than one hour, dude.
entries.attribution: Samuel Botero (F&F Humans V Robots Thu cohort)
entries.notes: Strong demo asset candidate. Low-pass filter audibly transformed the playback live. TODO: cut clip from https://fathom.video/share/MxD5y66n8ETNFjHWPeqSa2dLDkH_cYnu around 01:58:55-02:00:36 (~90s).

entries.title: Andrew went from 'super newbie' to 'set up with a good idea' in one session
entries.quote: I'm all set up now and I've got a good idea.
entries.attribution: Andrew Topping (F&F Humans V Robots Thu cohort)
entries.notes: Asked for the small-group breakout on call one. Right S1 outcome for a true beginner. Fathom: https://fathom.video/share/MxD5y66n8ETNFjHWPeqSa2dLDkH_cYnu

entries.title: Dominika ran her own build AND helped Andrew get unstuck
entries.quote: She's still here, she's just helping me with my computer.
entries.attribution: Andrew Topping describing Dominika (F&F Humans V Robots Thu cohort)
entries.notes: Multiplier behavior on session one — cohort needs more of this. Fathom: https://fathom.video/share/MxD5y66n8ETNFjHWPeqSa2dLDkH_cYnu

entries.title: Ruben pans the camera to show the in-person table at session open (hybrid reveal)
entries.quote: we've got a table now, Jess, you want to show... Ruben's going to show it up.
entries.attribution: Ruben Rosado (F&F Humans V Robots Thu cohort)
entries.notes: Clip cut and saved at build-room-visuals/clips/ff-thu-s1/ff-thu-s1-table-reveal.mp4 (91s, 16MB). Source span 00:00:59 → 00:02:30 of Fathom 158434606. Ruben is the camera operator panning the table; Alex narrates. Tail includes Cece's 'first Zoom since COVID' line at 00:02:27.

entries.title: Ruben panning the in-person table — 9s retreat-proof cut
entries.attribution: Ruben Rosado (F&F Humans V Robots Thu cohort)
entries.notes: 9-second cut from the 91s table-reveal clip (source span 52s-61s). Tile-cropped from Fathom gallery view, upscaled to 720p. Sent to F&F WhatsApp 2026-06-25 as a retreat tease. Pair with retreat-launch copy when that lands.
